title: System Design — URL Shortener
learningObjectives: Defend **URL shortener design** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why System Design — URL Shortener matters
sections.content: The **URL** **shortener** is a **classic** **interview** because it blends **ID** **generation**, **storage**, **caching**, and **analytics** **without** **drowning** in **domain** **complexity**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - System Design — URL Shortener
sections.content:
### Day 82 — **URL** **shortener**

### 1. API
**create**, **resolve**, optional **delete**/**analytics**.

### 2. IDs
**Hash**, **counter**, **hybrid** **collision** **handling**.

### 3. Storage
**KV** **primary**; **SQL** if **complex** **queries**.

### 4. Scale
**CDN** **edge** **cache** hot **links**.

### 5. Abuse
**Spam** **URL** **filter**, **rate** **limits**.

### 6. Story
**Viral** **link**—**p99** **redirect** **SLO** via **regional** **cache**.

sections.title: Basic — Core operations
Intermediate — Collision strategy
sections.output: on collision: append counter or rehash with salt

sections.title: Advanced — Hot link cache
sections.output: cache shortCode at edge; async click aggregate to stream

sections.title: Redirect flow
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 82 — Redirect
Browser -> API : GET /xYz
API -> Cache : lookup
Cache --> API : miss
API -> KV : get
KV --> API : long url
API --> Browser : 302
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — System Design — URL Shortener (Day 82 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: hash+truncate with collision check; counter+base62

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Hash** **based** **short** **code**?
conceptual.answer: Deterministic from URL—watch collisions; rehash or append salt on clash. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **URL shortener design**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Deterministic from URL—watch collisions; rehash or append salt on clash. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Deterministic from URL—watch collisions; rehash or append salt on clash. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

conceptual.question: **Counter** **+** **base62**?
conceptual.answer: Monotonic ID encoded compactly—needs distributed ID generation without hotspots. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: **Read** **vs** **write** **ratio** assumption?
conceptual.answer: Redirect path is extremely read-heavy—cache aggressively. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Piece | Recall |
|---|---|
| ID | hash or counter |
| Encode | base62 |
| Store | KV primary |
| Read | cache edge |
| Write | idempotent create |
| Abuse | rate limit |
| Analytics | async |
| 301/302 | cache semantics |
| Collision | check unique |
| Scale | partition by code |
